description: Primary dashboard for native Codex OpenTelemetry metrics emitted by codex_cli_rs, including project-aware progress, responsiveness, and friction signals.
title: Codex Native Telemetry Dashboard

widgets.description: Count of tool invocations made by Codex in the selected time range. A tool is an action Codex asks the host environment to perform, such as exec_command, write_stdin, apply_patch, request_user_input, or an MCP tool; this does not mean launching Codex itself.
widgets.title: Total Tool Calls

widgets.description: Count of permission prompts Codex created before running a tool or command that required approval. This measures human-intervention friction, not whether Codex accepted a code suggestion.
widgets.title: Approval Requests

widgets.description: Total model token usage reported by Codex turns in the selected time range, including input, cached input, output, reasoning output, and total token_type series where present.
widgets.title: Total Tokens

widgets.description: Count of low-level Codex client/server websocket events observed in the selected time range. This is protocol activity volume, not user-visible task progress.
widgets.title: Websocket Events

widgets.description: Time series of Codex tool invocations grouped by tool name. Tool names identify host actions Codex used, such as exec_command, write_stdin, apply_patch, request_user_input, or MCP tool calls.
widgets.title: Tool Calls Over Time

widgets.description: Time series of model tokens consumed by Codex turns, grouped by token_type. Token types include input, cached_input, output, reasoning_output, and total when emitted.
widgets.title: Token Usage Over Time

widgets.description: Distribution of Codex tool invocations by tool name over the selected time range. This shows which host capabilities Codex used most often, not which Codex model was used.
widgets.title: Tool Calls By Tool

widgets.description: Distribution of token usage by token_type over the selected time range. Use this to compare prompt/context tokens, cached input, generated output, and reasoning output.
widgets.title: Tokens By Type

widgets.description: Distribution of approval prompts by result, such as approved, approved_with_amendment, approved_for_session, or denied when present. This shows how often Codex needed and received permission to continue.
widgets.title: Approval Outcomes

widgets.description: Distribution of low-level websocket event kinds emitted by Codex. This is mainly useful for protocol/debug inspection; high counts do not necessarily mean more productive work.
widgets.title: Websocket Events By Kind

widgets.description: Global percentage for the selected time range: approval requests divided by total Codex tool invocations, multiplied by 100. This summarizes how often Codex needed human permission before it could use a host tool.
widgets.title: Approval Request Ratio

widgets.description: Global percentage for the selected time range: successful Codex tool invocations divided by all Codex tool invocations, multiplied by 100. This measures whether Codex host actions completed successfully; it does not prove the overall user task succeeded.
widgets.title: Tool Success Rate

widgets.description: Time series of Codex conversation turns. A turn is one user request/assistant response cycle tracked by Codex telemetry, so this is a better progress signal than raw websocket event volume.
widgets.title: Conversation Turns Over Time

widgets.description: Count of Codex threads or sessions started, grouped by project.name. Project name comes from OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES captured when Codex starts; the dashboard still shows all projects by default.
widgets.title: Threads Started By Project

widgets.description: Conversation turns grouped by project.name. This shows where Codex effort was spent across repositories or configured projects during the selected time range.
widgets.title: Turns By Project

widgets.description: Token usage grouped by project.name and token_type. Use this to see which project consumed model context/output/reasoning tokens, while remembering project context is captured from the Codex process environment.
widgets.title: Tokens By Project

widgets.description: Codex tool invocations grouped by project.name and tool. This shows which projects required more host actions such as shell commands, patches, stdin writes, approval prompts, or MCP calls.
widgets.title: Tool Calls By Project

widgets.description: Permission prompts grouped by project.name and approval outcome. This highlights which projects or tasks required more human approval before Codex could run tools.
widgets.title: Approvals By Project

widgets.description: Average end-to-end turn duration in milliseconds, calculated from codex.turn.e2e_duration_ms sum divided by count. This approximates how long a full Codex turn took from request to completion.
widgets.title: Average Turn Latency Over Time

widgets.description: Average time to first token in milliseconds, calculated from codex.turn.ttft.duration_ms sum divided by count. This measures how long it took before Codex began producing a response.
widgets.title: Average TTFT Over Time

widgets.description: Average time to first meaningful response in milliseconds, calculated from codex.turn.ttfm.duration_ms sum divided by count. This is a responsiveness signal distinct from full turn completion time.
widgets.title: Average TTFM Over Time

widgets.description: Codex API request count grouped by HTTP/status label and success flag. This helps distinguish normal model/API traffic from failed or unusual API responses.
widgets.title: API Requests By Status

widgets.description: Percentage of Codex tool invocations that required a permission prompt, calculated as approval requests divided by total Codex tool calls. Higher values mean more human approval friction before Codex could act.
widgets.title: Approval Request Ratio

widgets.description: Failed Codex tool invocations grouped by tool name. A failure means the host action itself did not complete successfully, such as a non-zero command, failed patch application, closed stdin session, sandbox denial, or tool error; it does not necessarily mean Codex failed the overall task.
widgets.title: Tool Failures By Tool

widgets.description: Codex MCP call count grouped by MCP tool name and status. MCP calls are tool invocations routed through Model Context Protocol servers, such as documentation, GitHub, or other connector-backed helpers.
widgets.title: MCP Calls By Tool

widgets.description: Conversation-turn telemetry grouped by project.name to verify attribution is present. Empty or unexpected project names usually mean Codex was started without the project-aware OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES shell hook, or a long-running session moved across directories.
widgets.title: Project Context Health
